Not all that complicated,  lights siren's we change siren pitch at intersections we do slow down and yeld while we look and listen, also accross the country their are opticoms that control traffic, blocks ahead signaled from the emergency responding vehicles aids and chaging traffic flow. Alote goes into it, we just dont go flying blind at high speeds into intetsections. But yes we all are human and unfortunately mistakes happen and adrenaline is hard to control from time to time. comment: A lot of interesting theories here, everyone is blaming the SUV, and I understand why. However, as a first responder, I must share this. Yes, there are laws in all 50 states which require drivers to yield the right of way to responding emergency vehicles, however, when an emergency vehicle has to proceed through a red light, the driver must do so with extreme caution because traffic with the green has some right of way. The reality is the ambulance driver in this situation crossed this intersection a bit too quickly. Most departments require a full stop at a red to first verify that traffic with the green is yielding before proceeding on red, they didn’t which means the ambulance driver is mostly responsible for the accident. Emergency services must respond with due regard for public safety.
